intent: Analyse a CITE-seq dataset (paired RNA + ADT/protein). Use a small public CITE-seq dataset (e.g. 10x's 5k/10k PBMC CITE-seq, or a muon/scanpy built-in if available). Load BOTH modalities (muon/MuData), QC + normalize each (CLR for the ADT, standard for RNA), cluster, and show a UMAP coloured by cluster with a few key ADT markers overlaid.

blocks.text: I'll help you analyze a CITE-seq dataset. Let me start by finding an appropriate public dataset and the best recipe for this analysis.

input.query: MuData multimodal RNA protein loading normalization

blocks.text: The search didn't return matches, but I can see from the skills index that there is a CITE-seq recipe available. Let me read it directly:
Great! Now let me create a plan based on the CITE-seq best-practice recipe:

input.title: CITE-seq QC, normalization, clustering and visualization
input.summary: Load a public 10x PBMC CITE-seq dataset, QC and normalize both RNA and ADT modalities (CLR for protein), cluster jointly on RNA, and visualize with UMAP colored by cluster and ADT marker expression overlaid.
input.rationale: This follows the Single-cell Best Practices workflow for CITE-seq: multimodal handling via MuData, ADT-specific QC (detected proteins, background), CLR normalization for surface proteins, RNA QC + SCT normalization, joint clustering on RNA, and marker visualization.
input.assumptions:
<item>Using 10x Genomics public PBMC CITE-seq data (e.g., 10k_pbmc_protein_v3 or a built-in dataset from scanpy/muon)</item>
<item>muon and anndata are available for multimodal analysis</item>
<item>ADTs will be CLR-normalized (standard for surface protein markers); RNA will use standard Seurat-style normalization</item>
<item>Clustering and UMAP will be computed on the RNA modality (more genes = more stable signal)</item>
<item>Key ADT markers (e.g., CD8, CD4, CD19, CD14) will be visualized on the UMAP</item>
</assumptions>
<parameter name="steps">
[
  {
    "title": "Load public CITE-seq data",
    "description": "Fetch a public 10x PBMC CITE-seq dataset (10k or 5k) into a MuData object with RNA and ADT modalities.",
    "expected_outputs": ["MuData object with RNA and ADT modalities", "Summary of cell count, gene count, ADT markers"]
  },
  {
    "title": "QC the RNA modality",
    "description": "Standard scRNA-seq QC: nUMI, nGenes, mitochondrial%, ribosomal%; apply cutoffs for low-quality cells.",
    "expected_outputs": ["QC metrics plots (UMI/gene/mito distributions)", "Filtered counts per modality"]
  },
  {
    "title": "QC the ADT modality",
    "description": "ADT-specific QC: nDetectedProteins, protein background (dsb or simple thresholding); remove cells with too few or anomalous protein signals.",
    "expected_outputs": ["ADT detection metrics", "Protein background assessment"]
  },
  {
    "title": "Normalize RNA (SCT-like normalization)",
    "description": "Apply log-normalization and scaling (or SCTransform), compute highly variable genes.",
    "expected_outputs": ["HVG set (e.g., 2000 genes)", "Normalized RNA counts"]
  },
  {
    "title": "Normalize ADT (CLR)",
    "description": "Apply centered log-ratio (CLR) normalization to ADT counts.",
    "expected_outputs": ["CLR-normalized ADT matrix"]
  },
  {
    "title": "Compute PCA and UMAP on RNA",
    "description": "PCA on HVGs, followed by UMAP; use RNA modality as the primary signal for dimensionality reduction.",
    "expected_outputs": ["PCA matrix", "UMAP coordinates"]
  },
  {
    "title": "Leiden clustering on RNA",
    "description": "Build kNN graph on PCA, apply Leiden algorithm.",
    "expected_outputs": ["Cluster assignments (per cell)", "Leiden clustering parameters used"]
  },
  {
    "title": "Visualize: UMAP by cluster + ADT markers",
    "description": "Plot UMAP colored by Leiden cluster; overlay key ADT markers (CD4, CD8, CD19, CD14, CD16 etc.) as separate colormaps.",
    "expected_outputs": ["Main UMAP cluster plot", "Panel of ADT marker UMAPs", "Figure showing immune cell identity"]
  }
]
